title: symphyotrichum praealtum var. texicola
intro: Symphyotrichum praealtum var. texicola, also known as Texas tallbone or Texicola prairie aster, is a perennial plant species in the daisy family, characterized by its tall stalks and white to pink flowers. It has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of Symphyotrichum praealtum var. texicola include infusions, teas, and salves, which are often used to promote overall well-being and treat various health conditions.
